source: IEA 2026 Energy Crisis Policy Response Tracker
context: Policy responses to energy market impacts of Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure, March 2026

policies.measure: Odd-even no-driving mandate for public sector workers by license plate number; private companies voluntarily participating
Capped air-conditioning temperatures in public buildings at 25°C, temporarily closed universities, reduced lighting use
Expanded public transport access to reduce private vehicle use
Temporarily reduced fuel taxes to lower pump prices for consumers
Imposed price caps on gasoline and diesel at retail level
Imposed price caps on gasoline and diesel at retail level
Restricted fuel purchases to 50 litres per day for private vehicles and 200 litres per day for companies and priority users
Introduced price caps and tax cuts on fuel at retail level
Victoria and Tasmania made public transport free for fixed time periods to reduce private vehicle use
Reduced highway speed limits nationally; free public transport in cities including Islamabad for one month
Government workers told to use stairs instead of elevators, wear short-sleeved shirts, work from home when possible; AC set to 26-27°C in government offices
Four-day work week for some government offices; non-essential office lights off during breaks and after hours; AC limited to 24°C or higher
Reduced VAT on fuel from 21% to 10%; eliminated 5% tax on electricity
Cut excise duties on fuel to reduce retail prices
Finance ministry examining windfall tax on oil companies to shield consumers from rising fuel prices
Energy contingency plan activated including temporary reduction in national speed limits to conserve fuel
Requested businesses and households to reduce energy consumption; extended Cool Biz dress code to year-round
Extended fuel subsidies for gasoline and electricity to cushion price impact on consumers
Cut excise duty on petrol and diesel; directed state oil companies to absorb partial price increases
Encouraged work-from-home for government and IT sectors; promoted public transport use in major cities
Introduced fuel price vouchers for low-income households; temporary fuel tax rebate
Lowered heating temperature mandate in public buildings to 19°C; reduced public lighting hours
Released strategic petroleum reserve barrels; suspended federal fuel tax temporarily
Federal agencies directed to reduce energy consumption by 15%; expanded telework for federal employees
Ordered energy-intensive industries to curtail production during peak hours; accelerated coal-to-gas switching
Capped retail fuel prices using existing pricing mechanism; released state oil reserves
Reduced special consumption tax on fuel; subsidized public transport fares
Reduced public building operating hours; mandated energy audits for large commercial buildings
Expanded household energy subsidies; introduced price cap on natural gas for residential use
Temporarily reduced highway speed limit from 130 to 100 km/h nationwide
Reduced VAT on fuel from 21% to 6%; extended energy cheques for vulnerable households
Introduced fuel price cap mechanism; tax rebate on fuel for professional transport operators
Increased fuel subsidies to prevent retail price increases; allocated emergency budget for energy
Government offices shifted to four-day work week; restricted AC usage in public buildings
Maintained existing fuel subsidies despite rising crude costs; targeted subsidy to B40 income group
Reduced environmental tax on fuel; used price stabilization fund to cap petrol prices
Provided U-Save energy rebates; temporary road tax reduction for commercial vehicles
Temporary speed limit reductions on state highways; promoted carpooling campaigns
Temporarily zeroed federal fuel taxes (PIS/COFINS); expanded Auxilio Gas program for low-income families
Increased fuel stimulus (estímulo fiscal) to cap gasoline prices; expanded Pemex domestic supply
Temporarily suspended federal carbon levy on gasoline; targeted rebates for rural and remote communities
Encouraged telework across federal public service; promoted EV charging incentives
Reduced general fuel levy and Road Accident Fund levy; emergency price monitoring at retail stations
Reintroduced partial fuel subsidies on petrol; allocated emergency funds for CNG bus fleet expansion
Used petroleum development levy stabilization fund to cap pump prices; waived import duty on LPG
Reduced public building lighting and operating hours; mandatory AC temperature floors in government buildings
Activated MEPCO fuel price stabilization mechanism at maximum subsidy; extended winter energy bonus
Froze domestic fuel price adjustments through price stabilization fund (FEPC)
Extended fuel price cap scheme for gasoline and diesel; increased social energy voucher value
Temporarily reduced excise duty on diesel and petrol; expanded household energy allowance
Reintroduced regulated fuel price caps; restricted fuel exports to ensure domestic supply
Reduced motorway speed limit to 100 km/h; expanded climate ticket (Klimaticket) access and subsidies
Issued voluntary energy savings appeal to households and businesses; reduced heating in federal buildings
Temporarily lowered highway speed limits; promoted cycling infrastructure and public transit incentives
Extended electricity price support scheme for households; increased oil production to assist global supply
Temporarily reduced fuel distribution obligation fee; targeted energy aid for low-income households
Reduced excise duty on petrol and diesel; extended electricity credit scheme
Mandated 20% energy reduction in government buildings; accelerated domestic gas field production
Maintained domestic fuel price caps; increased OPEC+ production to help stabilize global supply
Capped domestic fuel prices despite global increases; expanded energy subsidy for nationals
